steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The preceding sentence claims the event “used to be much smaller and more limited geographically.” The blank sentence then introduces the 1950 World Cup with only 13 teams—a specific historical case that illustrates this claim.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example: the second sentence gives a specific historical case (13 teams in 1950) that illustrates the general claim that the World Cup "used to be much smaller." The blank needs a transition word signaling that the following sentence is a specific instance of the preceding claim.
  • Choice A — In addition,: signals an extra parallel point, so it fails because the 1950 case illustrates the claim rather than adding a new one. ✘
  • Choice B — However,: signals contrast, so it fails because the second sentence supports, not contradicts, the claim about smaller scale. ✘
  • Choice C — At last,: signals something finally happening after a wait, so it fails because there is no sense of culmination here. ✘
  • Choice D — For example,: signals that a specific instance follows to illustrate a general claim, so it fits—the 1950 tournament with 13 teams is a concrete example of the event being "smaller and more limited." ✓
D is correct.
